description: Eval fixtures for the adaptive-run-classifier (Phase 5 of docs/plans/2026-06-03-adaptive-run-classifier/plan.md). Each fixture exercises one of the three task_class branches: vendor-docs, contested-comparison, open-web-general. Baseline+adaptive pairs are run per fixture; the harness compares metrics + LLM-judge-rated synthesis quality.

fixtures.question: How does Power Platform solution-export handle managed vs unmanaged for a customer-column polymorphism case where the column was upgraded from a lookup to a customer field after the dependent flow was authored? Walk the export-import asymmetry and what survives a re-import of the unmanaged form.
fixtures.notes: The originating PP-description run; baseline cost is the empirical anchor that motivated the whole plan. Phase 5 target: subagent_tokens ≤ 2.4M. Settles claim #11 (MCP vs WebFetch token ratio) via per-fetch token counts.

fixtures.question: Cube vs Metabase for a 5-tenant SaaS analytics embed — compare cost, row-level security model, embed/iframe story, customer-facing customization, and operational burden at 5 tenants growing toward 50. Lean on practitioner reports + vendor docs; flag where they disagree.
fixtures.notes: Contested comparison: vendor docs disagree with practitioner blogs; adaptive should pick balanced verify_default + top synthesize. Programmatic target: subagent_tokens adaptive ≤ baseline × 0.6 (lighter than vendor-docs target — contested costs more verify cardinality).

fixtures.question: What are the practical failure modes of the 2026 EU AI Act for downstream open-source AI deployers (e.g. small ISVs deploying a fine-tuned Llama)? Surface 5–8 concrete failure modes, each with the article/recital it derives from and an authoritative analysis cite.
fixtures.notes: Open-web regulated topic — regulation text + a few authoritative analyses, no specialized MCP. Adaptive should keep verify_default cheap (fast), escalate synthesize_contested to top. Programmatic target: subagent_tokens adaptive ≤ baseline × 0.5.
